name: Red Sea / Suez Canal
summary: Day 90 (Saturday May 23). **HOUTHI HAIFA 'MARITIME BLOCKADE' DAY 5 OF 14 — RHETORIC-ONLY TAG HOLDS; NO CONFIRMED KINETIC ATTACK ON HAIFA-BOUND VESSEL MAY 20-23 IN MAJOR MARITIME TRACKING OUTLETS. LLOYD'S JWC DAY 5 OF 7 WITH NO EASTERN MED LISTING CHANGE — INSURANCE MARKET NOT BUYING THE THREAT.** The Loadstar May 22-23 introduces a new tangent — Houthis may target 'Egypt's Red Sea ports' — but remains rhetoric only without operational confirmation. Lloyd's JWLA-033 (March 3, 2026) remains current published listing; no Eastern Med addition through May 23. AWRP ~1% holding from May 19 levels (8x pre-war). **The Houthi proxy network continues to demonstrate graduated restraint inside the Gulf-mediated deal window** — Iran-aligned signal that rhetoric coexists with deal architecture. Container freight: no fresh FBX print (Freightos terminal pull needed Monday). France Charles de Gaulle CSG continues Red Sea presence. **Negative-space verification holds**: no kinetic Red Sea incident May 20-23 beyond the May 11 unclaimed Hodeidah event. PRIOR (May 22): **HOUTHI HAIFA 'MARITIME BLOCKADE' DAY 4 OF 14 — RHETORIC-ONLY TAG HOLDS; NO CONFIRMED KINETIC ATTACK ON HAIFA-BOUND VESSEL; LLOYD'S JWC HAS NOT ADDED EASTERN MED.** Three days after the May 19 announcement (Houthi spokesman Saree), no confirmed kinetic attack on a Haifa-bound vessel has surfaced in major maritime tracking outlets (Maritime Executive, Ship & Bunker, Seatrade Maritime, Supply Chain Brain). **Lloyd's Joint War Committee Listed Areas update JWLA-033 (March 3, 2026)** added Bahrain/Djibouti/Kuwait/Oman/Qatar to Persian Gulf coverage — but NO subsequent update has added Eastern Mediterranean or Haifa. **Lloyd's JWC Eastern Med trigger remains `watching` Day 4 of 7**; confirmed kinetic attack trigger remains `watching` Day 4 of 14. **AWRP ~1% holding from May 19 levels** (Argus, Lloyd's List) — insurers have NOT bought the deal-anticipation tape yet; physical premium intact. **The Houthi proxy network is demonstrating graduated restraint inside the Gulf-mediated deal window** — Iran-aligned signal that rhetoric can coexist with deal architecture. Container freight: no fresh FBX print in this window. France Charles de Gaulle CSG continues Red Sea presence per prior. **The negative-space verification holds**: no kinetic Red Sea incident May 19-22 beyond the May 11 unclaimed Hodeidah event. PRIOR (May 19): **A4 NEGATIVE-SPACE WINDOW DAY 7 OF 7 CLOSES UNTRIPPED** — no second incident confirmed since May 11 Hodeidah; UKMTO recent-incidents page shows no new advisory through Monday morning IST; MARAD 2026-006 stands without supplement. **May 11 Hodeidah unclaimed incident reverts to 'single unclaimed event,' not start of new pattern.** Trigger on strait-of-hormuz node ('Second Red Sea shipping incident within 7 days of May 11 Hodeidah-area UKMTO advisory') moves watching → untripped_expired. **Houthi non-action during publicly-hardening US kinetic-preparation phase is the cleanest behavioural divergence from March-April pattern**: NYT preparation report May 16, Trump 'clock is ticking' May 17, Trump-Netanyahu call May 17, Saudi drone interception May 17, UAE Barakah drone strike May 17. Pair with TASS/Xinhua non-amplification + Mojtaba Day 11 silence + Hezbollah calibrated attrition = **coordinated quietism across Iran's proxy and patron networks against US kinetic noise**. Investor read: Iran signalling escalation control, not escalation. Container freight FBX01 Asia-US WC ~+2% w/w; Asia-US East Coast +10% same week; Asia-N Europe -3%; Asia-Med +7%. France Charles de Gaulle CSG continues Red Sea presence per prior. **PRIOR (May 17): Day 85 (Sunday). NEGATIVE-SPACE HOLDS DAY 6 OF 7 since May 11 Hodeidah unclaimed incident — UKMTO 'Recent Incidents' page shows NO second incident through May 17 morning IST.** If no incident by ~May 18 (Day 7), May 11 reverts to 'single unclaimed event' rather than start of new pattern. **The Houthi non-action persists through the weekend during which Trump-Iran is publicly hardening — Houthi quiet during a US kinetic-preparation phase is structurally significant intelligence, not coincidence.** Container freight FBX index update not available through weekend (weekly cadence). France Charles de Gaulle CSG continues Red Sea presence per prior. **PRIOR (May 13):** Day 81 — non-event POSSIBLY BROKEN May 11. UKMTO reported multiple explosions near a vessel ~130 km SW of Hodeidah; vessel continued voyage, no injuries; Houthis did NOT claim (CONFIRMED ACTION + UNCONFIRMED attribution). First Red Sea incident with possible Houthi attribution since Iran war's 2026 phase began. UKMTO maintains 'moderate' (not critical) for Bab el-Mandeb pending attribution. **NEW TRIGGER WATCH: second Red Sea shipping incident within 7 days of May 11 would collapse the Houthi non-action read.** Until then: single incident, sample size 1. France's Charles de Gaulle CSG remains in Red Sea / Gulf of Aden following CMA CGM San Antonio strike — first European naval-presence shift of cycle holds. Container freight FBX: Asia-USWC ~$2,127/FEU; Med ~$3,850/FEU (routing-penalty intact). Soufan coordination-decay thesis under first material test in 80 days but not yet falsified.

active_triggers.condition: Houthis attack commercial shipping in the Red Sea

recent_signals.headline: **HOUTHIS ANNOUNCED 'MARITIME BLOCKADE' OF HAIFA — CONFIRMED RHETORIC, NO ACTION; REVERSES MONDAY'S DAY 7 NEGATIVE-SPACE UNTRIPPED FRAMING.** Spokesman Saree said Haifa 'has, from the moment of this statement's release, become one of our targets'; Houthi Communications Center for Red Sea Traffic added a 'long-distance naval blockade' would apply. The announcement is framed as response to Israeli Gaza operations, NOT Iran-US kinetic preparation — preserving Houthi optionality in the Iran-US theater. **Critical structural observation**: Haifa is on the Israeli Mediterranean coast, far from Houthi missile/drone reach from Yemen — the 'long-distance' framing is more rhetorical than operational. Red Sea negative-space window from May 11 Hodeidah closed UNTRIPPED on May 18 Day 7; the Saree announcement re-opens a Mediterranean-axis test rather than a Red Sea-axis re-escalation. No new UKMTO advisory on Red Sea shipping through Tuesday morning IST. **Atlantic Council / Stimson framing**: 'partial regional ceasefires merely redirect rather than resolve hostilities.' Lloyds JWC EastMed AWRP movement will be the cleanest test of whether shipping community is pricing the Saree claim as credible.
recent_signals.verification: Houthi Haifa announcement = CONFIRMED RHETORIC (multi-source primary); operational reach to Mediterranean = REPORTED CLAIM not confirmed by independent maritime tracking; Red Sea negative-space holds — no new UKMTO advisory.

recent_signals.headline: **A4 NEGATIVE-SPACE DAY 7 OF 7 CLOSES UNTRIPPED** — no second incident confirmed since May 11 Hodeidah; UKMTO recent-incidents page shows no new advisory through Monday morning IST; MARAD 2026-006 stands without supplement. May 11 incident reverts to 'single unclaimed event,' not start of new pattern. Trigger on strait-of-hormuz node moves watching → untripped_expired. **Houthi non-action during publicly-hardening US kinetic-preparation phase is the cleanest behavioural divergence from March-April pattern** (NYT preparation, Trump 'clock is ticking', Trump-Netanyahu call, Saudi drone intercept, UAE Barakah strike all on the same weekend). Pair with TASS/Xinhua non-amplification + Mojtaba Day 11 silence + Hezbollah calibrated attrition = coordinated quietism across Iran's proxy and patron networks against US kinetic noise. **Investor read: Iran signalling escalation control, not escalation.** Container freight FBX01 Asia-US WC ~+2% w/w; Asia-US East Coast +10% same week; Asia-N Europe -3%; Asia-Med +7%. France Charles de Gaulle CSG continues Red Sea presence per prior.
recent_signals.verification: Day 7 untripped = CONFIRMED ABSENCE (UKMTO operational + MARAD official); coordinated-quietism across Iran network = CONFIRMED multi-source analytical observation (Houthi + Mojtaba + Hezbollah + TASS/Xinhua); FBX container rates = REPORTED (single-source weekly cadence).

recent_signals.headline: DAY 81 — RED SEA NON-EVENT POSSIBLY BROKEN MAY 11. UKMTO advisory recorded multiple explosions near a vessel ~130 km SW of Hodeidah on Monday May 11; vessel continued voyage, no injuries (CONFIRMED ACTION). **Houthis did NOT claim — attribution UNCONFIRMED.** First Red Sea shipping incident with possible Houthi attribution since the cycle began. New trigger window opens (7 days from May 11) — a second incident would collapse the Houthi non-action read. UKMTO 'moderate' threat level holds pending attribution. France's Charles de Gaulle CSG geometry unchanged.

FRANCE CHARLES DE GAULLE CSG TRANSITED SUEZ TO RED SEA / GULF OF ADEN FOLLOWING CMA CGM SAN ANTONIO STRIKE — FIRST EUROPEAN NAVAL SHIFT OF CYCLE; Macron called for Hormuz reopening AND spoke with Iranian President Pezeshkian (CONFIRMED — French armed forces, Naval News, ABC/NBC, US News, Washington Times). Paris: 'defensive step in full respect of international law' to 'reassure regional partners and support maritime security.' Day 76-77 of Houthi quiet sustained. FBX Med ~$3,850/FEU (+15% week — routing-penalty intact).
recent_signals.verification: CONFIRMED — French CSG redeployment multi-source (French primary + Western corroboration); Houthi quiet = CONFIRMED ABSENCE (UKMTO, Lloyd's List)

historical_summaries.summary: 1 signals. Houthis entered the war with a missile at Israel on March 28 but have NOT attacked Red Sea shipping (yet)

13 signals. Day 33: STILL zero confirmed Houthi attacks on Red Sea commercial shipping | Day 35: Still zero confirmed Houthi attacks on Red Sea commercial shipping | Day 38 of war — zero confirmed Houthi Red Sea shipping attacks
